parsing_approach: Anchor mode deterministically lists the claimable units of a documentation file (headings, list items with continuation lines, table rows) with line spans. Claim extraction itself is model-driven (skill 10-doc-alignment) and must state each claim against these anchors; check mode then re-resolves every claim in doc-claims.json onto the repository: cited file inside the repo, span within the file, verbatim excerpt present in the span, confirmed claims mapped to nodes that exist in the semantic graph, conflict claim_refs resolvable.
extracts: anchor mode: one anchor per heading / list item / table row, with span and text (capped at 300 chars)
check mode: pass/fail per doc-claims.json with one error string per violated claim
known_limitations: Anchor extraction is markdown-oriented; plain-text and reStructuredText files only match the list-item heuristics.
Prose paragraphs are not anchored; claims read from a paragraph must cite its line span directly, and only the excerpt check guards them.
Whether an excerpt faithfully supports the stated claim text remains a human judgement; the checker proves location, not interpretation.
Setext-style (underlined) markdown headings are not recognized as anchors.
